__inputs.name: DS_PROMETHEUS
__inputs.label: Prometheus
__inputs.description: Prometheus datasource that receives gen_ai_* and neoncart_* metrics (the OTLP gateway in Grafana Cloud).

description: Comprehensive use-case-driven view of the ai-o11y-demo-apps demo. Every panel queries metrics with verified active data. Built 2026-05-23 to be the master AI o11y demo dashboard.

panels.description: Total LLM operation calls in the last hour, scoped to the selected agent/provider/model.
panels.title: Total Requests (1h)

panels.description: Sum of input + output tokens consumed across all selected LLM calls.
panels.title: Total Tokens (1h)

panels.description: Cumulative LLM API spend in USD across all selected agents.
panels.title: Total Spend (1h)

panels.description: 95th-percentile LLM call duration over the last 5 min.
panels.title: p95 Latency

panels.description: Percentage of LLM calls returning an error_type, over the last 5 min.
panels.title: Error Rate %

panels.description: Distinct AI agent names that made at least one call in the last 5 min.
panels.title: Active Agents

panels.description: Random-but-sticky routing — each conversation is hashed to one provider. Use this to confirm the gateway is exercising both Anthropic + Ollama.
panels.title: Requests/sec by Provider

panels.description: Weighted-pool model selection in action. Expect 6 Claude + 4 Ollama models to all be exercised over time (Opus tier is rare per the weights).
panels.title: Requests/sec by Model

panels.description: Per-agent throughput. nc-chatbot/nc-gift-finder serve NeonCart shoppers. sb-router routes to sb-{billing, tech-support, account-management}.
panels.title: Requests/sec by Agent

panels.description: Tail latency per agent. SB router calls are usually fast (just routing), specialists (sb-billing etc.) are slower because they answer.
panels.title: p95 Latency by Agent

panels.description: Compare model speed: small Ollama (qwen2.5:3b) fast, large Anthropic (claude-opus-*) slow. Useful for model-selection tradeoffs.
panels.title: p95 Latency by Model

panels.description: Input vs output token rate. AI o11y story: most calls are input-heavy (system prompt + tool defs + history), with thin output. Watch input/output ratio.
panels.title: Tokens/sec by Type

panels.description: Token throughput by model. Lets you see which models are doing the heavy lifting (and consuming budget).
panels.title: Tokens/sec by Model

panels.description: Per-agent spend rate. Helps spot agents that are burning budget disproportionately. Pair with the ROI panel below.
panels.title: Spend rate $/hr by Agent

panels.description: Cost per model — Anthropic models cost ~10–100x more than Ollama (which is ~free per Stephen's local GPU). Watch the demo make this tradeoff visible.
panels.title: Spend rate $/hr by Model

panels.description: Provider-level $/hr. Lets a customer see exactly what each provider costs them over a rolling window.
panels.title: Spend rate $/hr by Provider

panels.description: Tool invocation rate per agent. nc-chatbot has search_products + navigate_to_search + add_to_cart. nc-gift-finder has search_by_criteria + add_to_cart. SB specialists have search_runbook + create_ticket. Sigil SDK doesn't attach gen_ai_tool_name to this metric, so it's per-agent only.
panels.title: Tool Calls/min by Agent

panels.description: The headline AI o11y story: did each AI agent pay for itself in cart-value generated? Above 1.0 = profitable. Below 1.0 = bleeding. nc-* agents only (SB has no ATC events).
panels.title: ROI ratio (cart value / token cost) — 15m window

panels.description: Cumulative cart-value / cost ratio since the metric began emitting. Big number = AI agent is earning N times its cost. Red <1x = bleeding.
panels.title: Cumulative ROI multiple

panels.description: Side-by-side green (cart value) vs red (cost). Confirms ROI is real — lots of green flowing relative to red.
panels.title: Cart value $/hr vs Token cost $/hr (per agent)

panels.description: New shopping sessions/min broken down by user_domain (gmail.com / aol.com / yahoo.com / unknown for anonymous).
panels.title: Sessions starting per minute

panels.description: Manual vs ai_chatbot vs ai_gift_finder. The AI-source slices are what populate the ROI metric.
panels.title: Add-to-Cart by Source

panels.description: Realized revenue from /api/orders checkouts, projected to $/hr.
panels.title: Revenue rate $/hr (completed orders)

panels.description: Total NeonCart page views.
panels.title: Page Views (1h)

panels.description: Total text searches against /api/search.
panels.title: Search Queries (1h)

panels.description: Total single-product page hits.
panels.title: Product Views (1h)

panels.description: Completed checkouts.
panels.title: Transactions (1h)

panels.description: OTel-autoinstrument http_server metrics — request rate per pod. Confirms every Python service is receiving traffic and exporting OTel metrics.
panels.title: HTTP request rate by service

panels.description: Service tail latency. Spikes here usually mean the service is queueing on a slow downstream (e.g. waiting on an LLM call).
panels.title: HTTP p95 by service

options.content:
## Conversation-level drill-down

This dashboard shows aggregates. For per-conversation inspection (the prompt text, the assistant reply, every tool call, every model switch within a conversation), use the **Sigil AI Observability** plugin:

**[Open Sigil Conversations](/a/grafana-sigil-app/conversations)** — list of every recent conversation across all agents, with model/cost/latency per turn.

**[Open Sigil Analytics](/a/grafana-sigil-app/analytics)** — plugin-provided dashboard with eval scores, model breakdowns, and conversation thread viewer.

### What's instrumented (use cases covered by this demo)

- **Multi-model routing** — 6 Claude models (Haiku, Sonnet 4.5, Sonnet 4.6, Opus 4.1, Opus 4.6, Opus 4.7) + 4 Ollama models (qwen2.5:3b/7b/14b, llama3.1:8b). Sticky per conversation.
- **Multi-provider routing** — Anthropic cloud + Ollama local. Deterministic hash per session+conversation_id.
- **Tool use** — nc-chatbot: search_products / navigate_to_search / add_to_cart. nc-gift-finder: search_by_criteria / add_to_cart. sb-*: search_runbook / create_ticket.
- **Multi-turn conversations** — per-conversation history kept in memory (30m TTL, 20-turn cap) so follow-ups like "show me cheaper options" remember context.
- **AI ROI** — `neoncart_ai_attributed_revenue_usd_total` tracks cart-value generated by each AI agent vs LLM cost. The headline KPI.
- **The "mice" trap** — nc-chatbot's `search_products` tool deliberately queries a non-existent `species` column when the user asks about mice, producing a real DB error in the trace cascade. Try it by typing "show me mice" in the chat widget.
- **NeonCart conversion funnel** — session_starts → page_views → search_queries / product_views → add_to_cart → transactions → revenue. AI-vs-manual sliced via the `source` label.
- **Cost transparency** — `gen_ai_client_cost_usd_total` emitted client-side by the gateway with full attribution (agent, model, provider, user_id).

panels.description: Highest-volume SupportBot users in the last hour. Pivot-table of per-employee call count, input tokens, output tokens, and cumulative cost. Acme employees (@acme.com) only — NeonCart shoppers are filtered out via gen_ai_agent_name=sb-*.
panels.title: Top SB employees — calls / tokens / cost (1h)

panels.description: Call rate for the busiest SupportBot users. Smoothed over a 5m window.
panels.title: Top 10 SB employees — calls/min

panels.description: Token throughput per SupportBot user. Stacked area so it's clear who's contributing most to the team's token bill.
panels.title: Tokens/sec by SB employee (input + output)

panels.description: Distinct Acme employees who chatted with SupportBot in the last hour.
panels.title: Active SB employees (last 1h)

panels.description: Mean call count across active employees. High number = power users; low number = spread across many people.
panels.title: Avg calls per employee (1h)

panels.description: Average input+output tokens consumed per active employee in the last hour.
panels.title: Avg tokens per employee (1h)

panels.description: Mean LLM spend per active employee. Useful for chargeback / capacity planning.
panels.title: Avg cost per employee (1h)

title: ai-o11y-demo-apps — Use Cases
